input.q:
Research the import tariffs, duties, and trade measures that apply to importing aluminium (unwrought aluminium and aluminium sheet/foil used for packaging) into the United States and into the European Union, as of 2026.

For each of the US and the EU, return:
- Relevant HS/tariff codes for unwrought aluminium and aluminium foil/sheet
- Standard (MFN) duty rates
- Any additional measures: US Section 232 tariffs, anti-dumping or countervailing duties, quotas, and any recent changes in 2025-2026
- Country-specific exemptions or higher rates that materially affect sourcing decisions
- Any relevant sanctions affecting aluminium from specific origins (e.g. Russia)

Cite official sources where possible (USITC HTS, US Customs, EU TARIC, European Commission). Clearly date each figure and flag anything that changed recently or is subject to review.

Sourcing and citation rules (apply to every claim):
- For each factual claim, cite the specific source that directly states that claim. Never attach a citation that does not itself contain the claim.
- Never cite a document dated before an event as the source for that event; the citation's own date must be consistent with the claim.
- Strongly prefer primary and authoritative sources: official company filings and press releases, regulators and government bodies, courts, standards bodies, and reputable news or trade press. Do not base a factual claim on SEO/marketing blogs, content farms, or open B2B marketplace listings; use such sources only if nothing better exists, and then explicitly label the claim as low-confidence.
- Label every figure as reported, estimate, or forecast, and give its date or as-of period.
- For any dramatic, contested, or fast-moving event, calibrate your language to what is actually confirmed, distinguish confirmed facts from uncertain ones, note where sources disagree on dates or numbers, and anchor to the most authoritative source available.
- If you cannot find a solid source for a claim, say so explicitly instead of asserting it.

output.answer:
**Date:** July 02, 2026
**To:** Executive Management / Supply Chain Strategy
**Subject:** 2026 US and EU Aluminium Import Tariffs, Trade Measures, and Supply Chain Impacts

### 1. Executive Summary
As of mid-2026, importing unwrought aluminium and aluminium packaging products (sheet/foil) into the United States and the European Union requires navigating highly volatile, protectionist trade regimes. The US landscape is dominated by a June 2025 increase in Section 232 tariffs to 50% for most nations, an April 2026 restructuring that applies these duties to the full customs value of goods, and a complete embargo on Russian metal. The EU landscape is defined by the January 1, 2026, implementation of the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) definitive phase, which imposes significant carbon-linked financial liabilities on importers, alongside aggressive anti-dumping duties on Chinese products and a phased total ban on Russian aluminium set to culminate in 2027.

---

### 2. United States: Tariff and Trade Landscape

#### 2.1. Base Tariffs (MFN / Column 1 Rates)
Standard import duties for aluminium products are classified under the Harmonized Tariff Schedule (HTS) Chapter 76. Base rates remain relatively low, though they are largely superseded by Section 232 tariffs.
*   **Unwrought Aluminium (HTS 7601):** 
    *   Non-alloyed (HTS 7601.10.30, least dimension ≤9.5mm): 2.6% [HTS CHAPTER 76 ALUMINUM AND ARTICLES THEREOF](https://www.usitc.gov/publications/docs/tata/hts/bychapter/1000c76.pdf).
    *   Other non-alloyed (HTS 7601.10.60): Free [HTS CHAPTER 76 ALUMINUM AND ARTICLES THEREOF](https://www.usitc.gov/publications/docs/tata/hts/bychapter/1000c76.pdf).
    *   Alloys (HTS 7601.20.30, least dimension ≤9.5mm): 2.6%; Alloys with ≥25% silicon (HTS 7601.20.60): 2.1%; Other alloys (HTS 7601.20.90): Free [HTS CHAPTER 76 ALUMINUM AND ARTICLES THEREOF](https://www.usitc.gov/publications/docs/tata/hts/bychapter/1000c76.pdf).
*   **Aluminium Sheet/Plates (HTS 7606):** Non-alloyed (7606.11.30) and alloyed (7606.12.30) rectangular plates/sheets over 0.2 mm thick are subject to a 3% duty [HTS CHAPTER 76 ALUMINUM AND ARTICLES THEREOF](https://www.usitc.gov/publications/docs/tata/hts/bychapter/1000c76.pdf).
*   **Aluminium Foil (HTS 7607):** 
    *   Thickness ≤0.01 mm (7607.11.30): 5.8% [HTS CHAPTER 76 ALUMINUM AND ARTICLES THEREOF](https://www.usitc.gov/publications/docs/tata/hts/bychapter/1000c76.pdf).
    *   Thickness 0.01 mm–0.15 mm (7607.11.60): 5.3% [HTS CHAPTER 76 ALUMINUM AND ARTICLES THEREOF](https://www.usitc.gov/publications/docs/tata/hts/bychapter/1000c76.pdf).
    *   Thickness 0.15 mm–0.2 mm (7607.11.90): 3% [HTS CHAPTER 76 ALUMINUM AND ARTICLES THEREOF](https://www.usitc.gov/publications/docs/tata/hts/bychapter/1000c76.pdf).
    *   *Note:* A 5.9% general rate increase is scheduled to take effect on January 1, 2026, specifically for HTS codes 7607.11.60 and 7607.11.90 [HTS Code 7607.11.60 US import tariffs and quota - UNIS](https://www.unisco.com/hts/76071160).

#### 2.2. Section 232 Tariffs & 2025-2026 Restructuring
The US has aggressively expanded its Section 232 national security tariffs on aluminium:
*   **Rate Increases:** Effective June 4, 2025, Section 232 tariffs on aluminium and derivatives were increased from 25% to 50% ad valorem for most countries [Section 232 Tariffs on Steel and Aluminum – Executive Order on Increase of Tariff Rate | AWPA](https://www.awpa.org/2025/06/04/section-232-tariffs-on-steel-and-aluminum-executive-order-on-increase-of-tariff-rate/). The UK is a notable exception, retaining a 25% rate [Section 232 Update: 50% Tariff on Aluminum Imports – New Reporting and Classification Rules | Buckland Customs](https://www.buckland.com/news/section-232-update-50-tariff-on-aluminum-imports-new-reporting-and-classification-rules/).
*   **Customs Value Rule:** Effective April 6, 2026, Section 232 duties apply to the *full customs value* of the imported product, rather than just the metal content value [Federal Register :: Strengthening Actions Taken To Adjust Imports of Aluminum, Steel, and Copper Into the United States](https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2026/04/09/2026-06960/strengthening-actions-taken-to-adjust-imports-of-aluminum-steel-and-copper-into-the-united-states).
*   **Tiered Rates & US-Metal Thresholds:** An April 2, 2026, Presidential Proclamation structured duties into tiers: 50% for primary metal, 25% for certain derivatives, and a temporary 15% rate for specific industrial equipment through 2027 [Section 232 Tariffs on Steel and Aluminum | Congress.gov | Library of Congress](https://www.congress.gov/crs-product/IN12519). Furthermore, as of June 8, 2026, derivative products qualify for a reduced 10% tariff if they are made of at least 85% US-sourced metal by weight (reduced from a previous 95% threshold) [U.S. Adjusts Section 232 Tariffs on Aluminum, Steel and Copper](https://www.ghy.com/trade-compliance/us-adjusts-section-232-tariffs-on-aluminum-steel-and-copper-full-customs-value-now-applies/).
*   **Unknown Origin:** If the country of smelt/cast is reported as "unknown," a punitive 200% tariff is assessed [Section 232 Steel and aluminum import tariff | International Trade](https://www.craneww.com/knowledge-center/trade-advisory-notices/section-232-steel-and-aluminum-import-tariff/).

#### 2.3. The Section 122 Global Surcharge (Contested)
On February 24, 2026, the US implemented a temporary 10% global import surcharge under Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 [Trump Administration Imposes 10% Section 122 Tariff in Plan to Replace IEEPA Tariffs | White & Case LLP](https://www.whitecase.com/insight-alert/trump-administration-imposes-10-section-122-tariff-plan-replace-ieepa-tariffs). 
*   **Interaction with Aluminium:** Section 122 does *not* stack with Section 232; aluminium products subject to Section 232 are exempt from the 122 surcharge [Section 122 Tariff 2026: Rate, Exemptions & What Changed | Triangle Trade Intelligence](https://triangle-trade-intel.site/blog/section-122-tariff-what-importers-need-to-know). However, hundreds of low-metal-content derivatives removed from Section 232 coverage in April 2026 subsequently fell under the 10% Section 122 tariff [United States modifies steel, aluminum, and copper Section 232 tariffs | White & Case LLP](https://www.whitecase.com/insight-alert/united-states-modifies-steel-aluminum-and-copper-section-232-tariffs).
*   **Legal Status:** On May 7, 2026, the US Court of International Trade ruled the Section 122 tariffs unlawful. However, on May 12, 2026, an appellate court issued a stay, allowing Customs to continue collecting the 10% surcharge from all importers (except the specific plaintiffs) while the appeal proceeds [Section 122 Stay: Why Importers Still Pay the 10% | Falcone Global](https://falconeglobal.com/news/section-122-tariffs-court-ruling-appeal-and-stay/).

#### 2.4. Country-Specific Sanctions and AD/CVDs
*   **Russia:** The US maintains a 200% ad valorem tariff on Russian aluminium and derivatives, including products where any primary aluminium was smelted/cast in Russia [A Proclamation on Adjusting Imports of Aluminum Into the United States | The White House](https://bidenwhitehouse.archives.gov/briefing-room/presidential-actions/2023/02/24/a-proclamation-on-adjusting-imports-of-aluminum-into-the-united-states-4/). Furthermore, the Treasury Department outright prohibited the importation of Russian-origin aluminium produced on or after April 13, 2024 [United States and United Kingdom Take Action to Reduce Russian Revenue from Metals | U.S. Department of the Treasury](https://home.treasury.gov/news/press-releases/jy2249).
*   **USMCA (Canada/Mexico):** For qualifying USMCA derivatives, the 25% Section 232 duty applies only to the *non-U.S. content* value, provided the total effective duty is at least 15% [FURTHER ADJUSTING THE TARIFF REGIMES FOR IMPORTS OF ALUMINUM, STEEL, AND COPPER INTO THE UNITED STATES 11032](https://public-inspection.federalregister.gov/2026-11314.pdf).
*   **China:** While the US and China agreed in November 2025 to reduce certain bilateral tariffs from 125% to 10%, this reciprocal reduction *does not apply* to aluminium, which remains under Section 232 [China Tariff Update: November 4 Executive Orders Signal Temporary Easing, Not Policy Reversal - Law Offices of Snell & Wilmer](https://www.swlaw.com/publication/china-tariff-update-november-4-executive-orders-signal-temporary-easing-not-policy-reversal/). Additionally, Chinese aluminium foil faces historical AD/CVDs (AD up to 151.04%, CVD up to 115.73%) [Anti-dumping and countervailing duties on aluminum foil exported from China - Knowledge](https://www.cnaluminiumfoil.com/info/anti-dumping-and-countervailing-duties-on-alum-79869239.html), and new AD/CVD orders were issued in 2025 for Chinese disposable aluminium containers [U.S. to Impose AD/CVD on Chinese Aluminum Containers | CH Powell Company](https://www.chpowell.com/newsroom/us-to-impose-ad-cvd-on-chinese-aluminum-containers/).

---

### 3. European Union: Tariff and Trade Landscape

#### 3.1. Base Tariffs (TARIC Codes)
The EU utilizes a 10-digit TARIC coding system based on the 2026 Combined Nomenclature (CN) [Commission publishes the 2026 version of the Combined Nomenclature - Taxation and Customs Union](https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/news/commission-publishes-2026-version-combined-nomenclature-2025-10-31_en). 
*   **Unwrought Aluminium (7601):** Baseline import duties range from 3% for unalloyed aluminium to 6% for aluminium alloys [Column: Europe's aluminium industry has its own tariff problems | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/article/idUSKCN1TM1JU/). Unwrought aluminium is subject to specific quotas [HS Code for EU Aluminum with Exclusion | FreightAmigo](https://www.freightamigo.com/en/blog/hs-code/hs-code-for-eu-aluminum-with-exclusion-1754276404428/).
*   **Aluminium Sheet (7606):** Covers plates/sheets >0.2 mm thick (e.g., TARIC 7606 11 91 25) [COMMISSION IMPLEMENTING REGULATION (EU) 2021/582](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/PDF/?uri=CELEX:32021R0582).
*   **Aluminium Foil (7607):** Covers foil ≤0.2 mm thick (e.g., TARIC 7607 19 10 90) [7607 1910 90 - Aluminium foil](https://www.taricsupport.com/nomenclature/en/7607191090.html).

#### 3.2. Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) - Definitive Phase
The most significant change to EU aluminium imports is the definitive phase of CBAM, which launched on January 1, 2026, shifting from reporting-only to mandatory financial obligations [CBAM Definitive Phase 2026 Malaysian Manufacturers Exporting to EU Must Prepare Now - Bernard Business Consulting](https://bernardbc.com/cbam-definitive-phase-2026-malaysian-manufacturers-exporting-to-eu-must-prepare-now/).
*   **Compliance:** Importers of 7601, 7606, and 7607 products must be "authorised CBAM declarants" [EU’s Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism Enters Definitive Phase to Curb Carbon Leakage – ESG BROADCAST](https://esgbroadcast.com/broadcast/eus-carbon-border-adjustment-mechanism-enters-definitive-phase-to-curb-carbon-leakage/). A de minimis exemption applies for importers bringing in less than 50 tonnes annually [CBAM Explained 2026 | Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (EU) 2023/956 | GreenSutra®](https://greensutra.in/news/cbam-explained-2026/).
*   **Financial Liability:** Importers must purchase CBAM certificates linked to the EU ETS auction price (reported at €75.36 per tonne of CO2e in April 2026) [AI Blog for Governments and Enterprises | Net0](https://net0.com/blog/the-eu-carbon-border-adjustment-mechanism-cbam). In 2026, liability is moderated by a 97.5% free allocation deduction, which phases out to 0% by 2034 [CBAM - REYHER - Verbindungselemente und Befestigungstechnik](https://www.reyher.de/en/company/cbam/).
*   **Verification:** 2026 emissions data must be verified by an accredited third party, requiring on-site inspections during the first year [CBAM enters the definitive period: key regulatory updates and scope extension | carboneer](https://carboneer.earth/en/2025/12/cbam-definitive-period-scope-extension/). Non-compliance triggers penalties up to €100 per tonne of CO2e plus the cost of missing certificates [Comparing the UK and EU CBAM: Key Differences in Carbon Border Adjustment Policies - Redshaw Advisors](https://redshawadvisors.com/comparing-the-uk-and-eu-cbam-key-differences-in-carbon-border-adjustment-policies/).

#### 3.3. Sanctions and Safeguards
*   **Russia:** The EU is aggressively phasing out Russian aluminium. The 12th package (Dec 2023) banned wire, foil, and tubes [EU adopts 12th package of sanctions against Russia for its continued illegal war against Ukraine - Enlargement and Eastern Neighbourhood](https://enlargement.ec.europa.eu/news/eu-adopts-12th-package-sanctions-against-russia-its-continued-illegal-war-against-ukraine-2023-12-19_en). The 16th package (Feb 2025) banned unwrought aluminium (7601), allowing a transitional quota of 275,000 metric tons through February 26, 2026 [Questions and answers on the sixteenth package of restrictive measures against Russia - Enlargement and Eastern Neighbourhood](https://enlargement.ec.europa.eu/news/questions-and-answers-sixteenth-package-restrictive-measures-against-russia-2025-02-24_en). A secondary 50,000-ton quota applies to pre-existing contracts until December 31, 2026, after which a total ban takes effect in 2027 [Recent key sanctions imposed against Russia by the EU and the UK - Skuld](https://www.skuld.com/topics/legal/sanctions/russia/recent-key-sanctions-imposed-against-russia-by-the-eu-and-the-uk/).
*   **Norway & Iceland:** Despite their EEA status, the EU imposed new safeguards effective November 18, 2025. Imports are duty-free up to a quota of 75% of historical (2022-2024) volumes. Volumes exceeding this quota face a variable duty triggered if import prices fall below a specific price floor [Norway stung by new EU tariff | Norway's News in English](https://www.newsinenglish.no/2025/11/18/norway-stung-by-new-eu-tariff/).
*   **Mozambique:** Exempt from baseline duties under the Everything But Arms (EBA) initiative, but fully subject to CBAM carbon taxes starting in 2026 [EU carbon tax prompts "green aluminium" claims in Mozambique](https://www.climatechangenews.com/2023/07/03/mozambique-aluminium-cbam-carbon-border-tax/).

#### 3.4. Anti-Dumping and Countervailing Duties (AD/CVD)
The EU heavily targets Chinese aluminium imports with trade defense instruments:
*   **Flat-Rolled Products (7606/7607):** Definitive AD duties are set at a standard rate of 24.6% (individual company rates range from 14.3% to 21.4%). These are scheduled to expire on October 12, 2026, though a partial interim review was initiated in March 2026 [Trade and Industry Department - EU: Impending expiry of the anti-dumping measures on imports of aluminium flat-rolled products originating in Chinese Mainland](https://www.tid.gov.hk/en/tradecircular/2026/ci962026.html).
*   **Converter Foil (ex 7607 11 19):** AD/CVD rates range from 15.4% to 28.5%, expected to expire December 9, 2026 [EU duties on Chinese aluminium converter foil to expire in 2026](https://www.alcircle.com/news/eu-duties-on-chinese-aluminium-converter-foil-to-expire-in-2026-117829).

---

### 4. Supply Chain Impacts and Risk Assessment
The compounding effects of US tariffs and EU carbon taxes are fundamentally restructuring global aluminium flows. 
*   **Cost Premiums:** US buyers are currently paying a 68% premium over the London Metal Exchange (LME) price for physical metal as of January 2026 due to the combined impact of 50% Section 232 tariffs and global supply shifts [US aluminium consumers pay the spiralling cost of tariffs | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/markets/commodities/metals/us-aluminium-consumers-pay-spiralling-cost-tariffs-2026-01-21/).
*   **Trade Diversion:** Producers are redirecting export volumes away from North America toward the EU to capture higher margins, while Canadian and Mexican processors are heavily leveraging USMCA trade benefits to supply the US market [Global market faces tariff impact, reveals aluminium supply chain vulnerabilities](https://www.alcircle.com/news/market-faces-tariff-impact-reveals-aluminium-supply-chain-vulnerabilities-117266).
*   **Compliance Overhead:** EU importers face severe administrative burdens in 2026. Total CBAM costs for EU importers are estimated to exceed €12 billion in 2026 (roughly 15% of covered import value) [Provisional CBAM benchmarks announced: how will this restructure global trade? - Fastmarkets](https://www.fastmarkets.com/insights/provisional-cbam-benchmarks-announced-how-will-this-restructure-global-trade/). Sourcing from high-emitting countries without primary data forces the use of EU default values, which carry mark-ups of up to 30% [CBAM enters the definitive period: key regulatory updates and scope extension | carboneer](https://carboneer.earth/en/2025/12/cbam-definitive-period-scope-extension/).

---

### 5. Gaps and Limitations
*   **Exact EU MFN Rates for 7606/7607:** While the baseline MFN duty for unwrought aluminium (7601) is confirmed at 3-6%, the exact standard MFN percentage rates for sheet (7606) and foil (7607) are not explicitly detailed in the available findings, beyond the specific AD/CVD rates applied to China.
*   **US AD/CVD Current Rates:** The findings provide historical ranges for US AD/CVDs on Chinese foil (up to 151% AD / 115% CVD) and note new 2025 orders for containers, but do not provide the exact finalized 2026 AD/CVD percentages for these specific HTS codes.
*   **Section 122 Stacking Contradiction:** There is minor conflict in the trade press regarding whether the US Section 122 surcharge can ever stack with Section 232; while primary consensus indicates they do not stack, some consulting sources suggest potential combined exposure for certain edge-case products.

sources.name: HTS CHAPTER 76 ALUMINUM AND ARTICLES THEREOF
sources.snippet: For HTS 7601.10.30, which covers unwrought aluminum (not alloyed) of uniform cross section with the least dimension not greater than 9.5 mm in coils, the Column 1 General rate of duty is 2.6%.

sources.name: HTS CHAPTER 76 ALUMINUM AND ARTICLES THEREOF
sources.snippet: For HTS 7601.10.60, which covers other unwrought aluminum (not alloyed), the Column 1 General rate of duty is Free.

sources.snippet: For HTS 7601.20.30, which covers unwrought aluminum alloys of uniform cross section with the least dimension not greater than 9.5 mm in coils, the Column 1 General rate of duty is 2.6%.

sources.snippet: For HTS 7601.20.60, which covers unwrought aluminum alloys containing 25 percent or more by weight of silicon, the Column 1 General rate of duty is 2.1%.

sources.name: HTS CHAPTER 76 ALUMINUM AND ARTICLES THEREOF
sources.snippet: For HTS 7601.20.90, which covers other unwrought aluminum alloys, the Column 1 General rate of duty is Free.

sources.snippet: For HTS 7606.11.30, which covers non-alloyed aluminum plates, sheets, and strip (rectangular, not clad, thickness over 0.2 mm), the Column 1 General rate of duty is 3%.

sources.snippet: For HTS 7606.12.30, which covers aluminum alloy plates, sheets, and strip (rectangular, not clad, thickness over 0.2 mm), the Column 1 General rate of duty is 3%.

sources.snippet: For HTS 7607.11.30, which covers aluminum foil (not backed, rolled but not further worked, thickness not exceeding 0.01 mm), the Column 1 General rate of duty is 5.8%.

sources.snippet: For HTS 7607.11.90, which covers aluminum foil (not backed, rolled but not further worked, thickness exceeding 0.15 mm but not exceeding 0.2 mm), the Column 1 General rate of duty is 3%.

sources.name: HTS Code 7607.11.60 US import tariffs and quota - UNIS
sources.snippet: A 5.9% general rate increase is scheduled to take effect on January 1, 2026, for HTS codes 7607.11.60 and 7607.11.90.

sources.name: Commission publishes the 2026 version of the Combined Nomenclature - Taxation and Customs Union
sources.snippet: The 2026 version of the Combined Nomenclature (CN), which forms the basis for EU customs declarations and duty rates, is established by Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1926 and is applicable from 1 January 2026.

sources.name: 7607 1910 90 - Aluminium foil
sources.snippet: TARIC code 7607 19 10 90 is a complete 10-digit code for aluminium foil of a thickness not exceeding 0.2 mm.

sources.name: COMMISSION IMPLEMENTING REGULATION (EU) 2022/402
sources.snippet: Aluminium household foil (AHF) is specifically identified under TARIC code 7607 11 19 10.

sources.name: COMMISSION IMPLEMENTING REGULATION (EU) 2021/582
sources.snippet: Specific TARIC codes for aluminium plates and sheets (7606) include 7606 11 91 25, 7606 11 93 25, and 7606 11 99 25.

sources.name: Section 232 Tariffs on Steel and Aluminum – Executive Order on Increase of Tariff Rate | AWPA
sources.snippet: Effective June 4, 2025, Section 232 tariffs on aluminum articles and derivative products were increased from 25% to 50% ad valorem for most countries.

sources.name: Section 232 Update: 50% Tariff on Aluminum Imports – New Reporting and Classification Rules | Buckland Customs
sources.snippet: Aluminum imports from the United Kingdom are subject to a lower Section 232 tariff rate of 25%, with potential changes or quotas dependent on the U.S.-UK Economic Prosperity Deal starting July 9, 2025.

sources.name: Federal Register :: Strengthening Actions Taken To Adjust Imports of Aluminum, Steel, and Copper Into the United States
sources.snippet: Effective April 6, 2026, Section 232 duties on aluminum, steel, and copper articles and derivatives apply to the full customs value of the imported product, rather than just the metal content value.

sources.name: FURTHER ADJUSTING THE TARIFF REGIMES FOR IMPORTS OF ALUMINUM, STEEL, AND COPPER INTO THE UNITED STATES 11032
sources.snippet: For aluminum derivative products from Canada and Mexico qualifying for USMCA preferential treatment, the 25% Section 232 duty applies only to the non-U.S. content value, provided the total effective duty is at least 15%.

sources.name: U.S. to Impose AD/CVD on Chinese Aluminum Containers | CH Powell Company
sources.snippet: The USITC determined in 2025 that U.S. producers are materially injured by imports of disposable aluminum containers, pans, trays, and lids from China, leading to the issuance of new AD/CVD orders.

sources.name: Section 232 Steel and aluminum import tariff | International Trade
sources.snippet: Effective June 28, 2025, if the country of smelt and/or cast for aluminum products is reported as 'unknown' (UN), a 200% ad valorem tariff is assessed.

sources.name: FURTHER ADJUSTING THE TARIFF REGIMES FOR IMPORTS OF ALUMINUM, STEEL, AND COPPER INTO THE UNITED STATES 11032
sources.snippet: Aluminum lithographic plates (HTS 3701.30.00) were newly added to Section 232 derivative coverage at a 25% rate effective June 8, 2026.

sources.name: Anti-dumping and countervailing duties on aluminum foil exported from China - Knowledge
sources.snippet: Historical anti-dumping duties on aluminum foil from China have ranged from 35.77% to 151.04%, while countervailing duties have ranged from 5.06% to 115.73% depending on the investigation period and producer.

sources.name: A Proclamation on Adjusting Imports of Aluminum Into the United States | The White House
sources.snippet: Beginning April 10, 2023, the 200% tariff was expanded to include aluminum articles where any amount of primary aluminum used in manufacture is smelted or cast in Russia, regardless of the country of origin of the finished article.

sources.name: United States and United Kingdom Take Action to Reduce Russian Revenue from Metals | U.S. Department of the Treasury
sources.snippet: The U.S. Department of the Treasury prohibited the importation of Russian-origin aluminum, copper, and nickel produced on or after April 13, 2024, into the United States.

sources.name: United States and United Kingdom Take Action to Reduce Russian Revenue from Metals | U.S. Department of the Treasury
sources.snippet: Global metal exchanges, including the London Metal Exchange (LME) and Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME), are prohibited from accepting new aluminum produced by Russia as of April 2024.

sources.name: U.S. Adjusts Section 232 Tariffs on Aluminum, Steel and Copper
sources.snippet: Effective April 6, 2026, Section 232 tariffs apply to the full customs value of covered aluminum articles and derivatives, rather than just the value of the metal content.

sources.name: HS Code for EU Aluminum with Exclusion | FreightAmigo
sources.snippet: HS code 7601 for unwrought aluminium (bars, blocks, and primary forms) is subject to quotas in the European Union.

sources.name: HS Code for EU Aluminum with Exclusion | FreightAmigo
sources.snippet: HS code 7607 is designated for aluminium foil with a thickness of 0.2mm or less, while HS 7606 is for aluminium sheet.

sources.name: Trade and Industry Department - EU: Impending expiry of the anti-dumping measures on imports of aluminium flat-rolled products originating in Chinese Mainland
sources.snippet: The European Union applies definitive anti-dumping duties on imports of aluminium flat-rolled products originating in China at a standard rate of 24.6%, with individual company rates ranging from 14.3% to 21.4%.

sources.name: Trade and Industry Department - EU: Impending expiry of the anti-dumping measures on imports of aluminium flat-rolled products originating in Chinese Mainland
sources.snippet: Anti-dumping measures on Chinese aluminium flat-rolled products (under various CN codes in 7606 and 7607) are scheduled to expire at midnight on October 12, 2026, unless a review is initiated.

sources.name: EU duties on Chinese aluminium converter foil to expire in 2026
sources.snippet: EU anti-dumping and countervailing duties on Chinese aluminium converter foil (CN code ex 7607 11 19) range from 15.4% to 28.5% and are expected to expire on December 9, 2026.

sources.name: CBAM Definitive Phase 2026 Malaysian Manufacturers Exporting to EU Must Prepare Now - Bernard Business Consulting
sources.snippet: The definitive phase of the EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) officially commenced on January 1, 2026, transitioning from a reporting-only phase to one involving mandatory financial obligations.

sources.name: EU’s Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism Enters Definitive Phase to Curb Carbon Leakage – ESG BROADCAST
sources.snippet: EU importers of aluminium must obtain 'authorised CBAM declarant' status from national competent authorities to import in-scope goods into the EU customs territory starting in 2026.

sources.name: CBAM Explained 2026 | Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (EU) 2023/956 | GreenSutra®
sources.snippet: Importers are required to submit an annual CBAM declaration for 2026 imports by September 30, 2027, though one source notes a deadline of May 31, 2027.

sources.name: AI Blog for Governments and Enterprises | Net0
sources.snippet: From 2027, importers must hold CBAM certificates covering at least 50% of year-to-date embedded emissions at the end of each quarter.

sources.name: AI Blog for Governments and Enterprises | Net0
sources.snippet: The price of CBAM certificates is linked to the weekly average auction price of EU Emissions Trading System (ETS) allowances; the first certificate price published by the European Commission on April 7, 2026, was €75.36 per tonne of CO2e.

sources.name: CBAM Explained 2026 | Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (EU) 2023/956 | GreenSutra®
sources.snippet: Regulation (EU) 2025/2083 introduced a de minimis threshold exempting importers who bring in less than 50 tonnes of covered aluminium goods per calendar year from reporting and certificate surrender obligations.

sources.name: CBAM - REYHER - Verbindungselemente und Befestigungstechnik
sources.snippet: Financial liability is moderated by a 'CBAM factor' that mirrors the phase-out of free EU ETS allowances; in 2026, a 97.5% deduction applies, which will reach 0% by 2034.

sources.name: Comparing the UK and EU CBAM: Key Differences in Carbon Border Adjustment Policies - Redshaw Advisors
sources.snippet: Non-compliance with CBAM requirements triggers penalties of up to €100 per tonne of CO2e, in addition to the cost of purchasing the missing certificates.

sources.name: CBAM enters the definitive period: key regulatory updates and scope extension | carboneer
sources.snippet: Embedded emissions data for 2026 imports must be verified by an accredited independent third party, which requires verifiers to perform on-site inspections during the first year of reporting.

sources.name: CBAM - REYHER - Verbindungselemente und Befestigungstechnik
sources.snippet: Imports from Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein, and Switzerland are exempt from CBAM as they participate in the EU ETS or an equivalent carbon pricing system.

sources.name: CBAM enters the definitive period: key regulatory updates and scope extension | carboneer
sources.snippet: If actual emissions data is unavailable, importers must use EU-established default values, which include mark-ups of up to +30% for most CBAM products from 2028 onwards to reflect potential underestimation.

sources.name: Section 232 Tariffs on Steel and Aluminum | Congress.gov | Library of Congress
sources.snippet: In June 2025, President Trump increased Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum imports to 50%, with the exception of the United Kingdom which remained at 25% under the U.S.-UK Economic Prosperity Deal.

sources.name: U.S. Adjusts Section 232 Tariffs on Aluminum, Steel and Copper
sources.snippet: Effective April 6, 2026, Section 232 tariffs apply to the full customs value of covered metal articles and derivatives, rather than just the value of the metal content.

sources.name: Section 232 Tariffs on Steel and Aluminum | Congress.gov | Library of Congress
sources.snippet: The April 2026 restructuring established a tiered rate system: 50% for primary metal articles (Annex I-A), 25% for certain derivatives (Annex I-B), and a reduced rate of 15% for specific industrial and electrical grid equipment through 2027.

sources.name: U.S. Adjusts Section 232 Tariffs on Aluminum, Steel and Copper
sources.snippet: Effective June 8, 2026, the threshold for a product to qualify as made 'entirely' from American metal was modified from 95% to 85% to receive a reduced 10% tariff rate.

sources.name: US aluminium consumers pay the spiralling cost of tariffs | Reuters
sources.snippet: U.S. aluminum buyers are paying a 68% premium over the London Metal Exchange (LME) price for physical metal as of January 2026 due to the combined impact of tariffs and CBAM.

sources.name: Provisional CBAM benchmarks announced: how will this restructure global trade? - Fastmarkets
sources.snippet: Total CBAM costs for EU importers are estimated to exceed €12 billion in 2026, representing approximately 15% of the value of covered imports.

sources.name: Global market faces tariff impact, reveals aluminium supply chain vulnerabilities
sources.snippet: Producers are redirecting export volumes from North America toward higher-margin destinations like the European Union, while Canadian and Mexican processors are leveraging USMCA trade benefits.

sources.name: EU adopts 12th package of sanctions against Russia for its continued illegal war against Ukraine - Enlargement and Eastern Neighbourhood
sources.snippet: The EU's 12th sanctions package, adopted on December 18, 2023, imposed import bans on specific Russian aluminium products including wires, foil, tubes, and pipes.

sources.name: Questions and answers on the sixteenth package of restrictive measures against Russia - Enlargement and Eastern Neighbourhood
sources.snippet: A transitional quota mechanism under the 16th package allows for the import of 275,000 metric tons of Russian aluminium between February 25, 2025, and February 26, 2026.

sources.name: Recent key sanctions imposed against Russia by the EU and the UK - Skuld
sources.snippet: For contracts concluded before February 25, 2025, the EU allows a secondary import quota of up to 50,000 metric tons of Russian aluminium from February 26, 2026, until December 31, 2026.

sources.name: Questions and answers on the sixteenth package of restrictive measures against Russia - Enlargement and Eastern Neighbourhood
sources.snippet: The 16th package also added 53 new entities to the restricted list for supporting the Russian military-industrial complex or engaging in sanctions circumvention, including entities in China, India, and the UAE.

sources.name: Column: Europe's aluminium industry has its own tariff problems | Reuters
sources.snippet: EU baseline import duties for aluminium range from 3% for unalloyed aluminium to 6% for aluminium alloys.

sources.name: Norway stung by new EU tariff | Norway's News in English
sources.snippet: Under 2025 EU trade safeguards, 75% of historical metal alloy imports from Norway and Iceland remain duty-free, while imports exceeding that threshold are subject to protective tariffs.

sources.name: Column: Europe's aluminium industry has its own tariff problems | Reuters
sources.snippet: Mozambique is exempt from EU aluminium import duties due to its status under a preferential trade agreement and the Everything But Arms (EBA) initiative for Least Developed Countries.

sources.name: EU carbon tax prompts "green aluminium" claims in Mozambique
sources.snippet: Starting in 2026, Mozambique's aluminium exports to the EU will be subject to the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), which will tax carbon emissions at the border with no exemptions for LDCs.

sources.name: China Tariff Update: November 4 Executive Orders Signal Temporary Easing, Not Policy Reversal - Law Offices of Snell & Wilmer
sources.snippet: The reciprocal tariff reduction does not apply to aluminum; aluminum is subject to separate Section 232 tariffs which were not modified by the November 2025 Executive Orders.

sources.name: Section 232 Tariffs on Steel and Aluminum | Congress.gov | Library of Congress
sources.snippet: Section 232 tariffs on aluminum apply to the full customs value of the articles, including derivative products, as clarified in April 2026 proclamations.

sources.name: HS Code for EU Aluminum with Exclusion | FreightAmigo
sources.snippet: EU TARIC code 7601 refers to unwrought aluminium, which includes bars, blocks, and primary forms.

sources.name: HS Code for EU Aluminum with Exclusion | FreightAmigo
sources.snippet: EU TARIC code 7606 refers to aluminium plates, sheets, and strip of a thickness exceeding 0.2 mm.

sources.name: HS Code for EU Aluminum with Exclusion | FreightAmigo
sources.snippet: Unwrought aluminium under HS 7601 is subject to quotas for primary forms such as bars and blocks.

sources.name: HS Code for EU Aluminum with Exclusion | FreightAmigo
sources.snippet: The 2025 EU Combined Nomenclature updates included added recycling specifications for 7606 and sustainability notes for foil (7607).

sources.name: Trump Administration Imposes 10% Section 122 Tariff in Plan to Replace IEEPA Tariffs | White & Case LLP
sources.snippet: A temporary 10% ad valorem import surcharge under Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 was implemented effective 12:01 a.m. EST on February 24, 2026.

sources.name: Trump Administration Imposes 10% Section 122 Tariff in Plan to Replace IEEPA Tariffs | White & Case LLP
sources.snippet: The Section 122 surcharge is not additive with sectoral tariffs imposed under Section 232, meaning it generally does not stack on top of existing Section 232 duties.

sources.name: Section 122 Tariff 2026: Rate, Exemptions & What Changed | Triangle Trade Intelligence
sources.snippet: Aluminum products (HTS chapter 76) are exempt from the Section 122 surcharge because they are already subject to Section 232 tariffs.

sources.name: United States modifies steel, aluminum, and copper Section 232 tariffs | White & Case LLP
sources.snippet: A presidential proclamation on April 2, 2026, removed hundreds of products with low metal content from the scope of Section 232; these products subsequently became subject to the 10% Section 122 global tariff.

sources.name: Norway stung by new EU tariff | Norway's News in English
sources.snippet: The tariff-rate quota (TRQ) for these imports is set at 75% of the historical average import volumes from the period 2022–2024.

sources.name: HS Code for EU Aluminum with Exclusion | FreightAmigo
sources.snippet: Aluminium plates, sheets, and strip with a thickness exceeding 0.2 mm are classified under HS code 7606.

sources.name: HS Code for EU Aluminum with Exclusion | FreightAmigo
sources.snippet: Aluminium foil with a thickness not exceeding 0.2 mm is classified under HS code 7607.

sources.name: EU duties on Chinese aluminium converter foil to expire in 2026
sources.snippet: The European Union imposes definitive anti-dumping and countervailing duties ranging from 15.4% to 28.5% on Chinese aluminium converter foil under CN code ex 7607 11 19.

sources.name: COMMISSION IMPLEMENTING REGULATION (EU) 2025/1720 of 6 August 2025
sources.snippet: Aluminium foil with a thickness between 0.007 mm and 0.021 mm, not backed, in rolls not exceeding 10 kg, falls under TARIC codes 7607 11 11 11, 7607 11 11 19, and 7607 19 10 11.

sources.name: HS Code for EU Aluminum with Exclusion | FreightAmigo
sources.snippet: Recycling-grade aluminium under heading 7606 qualifies for anti-dumping duty exclusions that are updated on a quarterly basis.

sources.name: Section 122 Stay: Why Importers Still Pay the 10% | Falcone Global
sources.snippet: On May 12, 2026, the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit issued an administrative stay of the CIT decision, allowing U.S. Customs and Border Protection to continue collecting the 10% Section 122 surcharge from all importers except the named plaintiffs while the appeal proceeds.

sources.name: United States modifies steel, aluminum, and copper Section 232 tariffs | White & Case LLP
sources.snippet: A presidential proclamation issued on April 2, 2026 (effective April 6, 2026) removed hundreds of steel and aluminum derivative products from the scope of Section 232 actions, specifically those listed in Annex II of the proclamation.

sources.name: United States modifies steel, aluminum, and copper Section 232 tariffs | White & Case LLP
sources.snippet: Aluminum derivative products removed from Section 232 coverage by the April 2026 proclamation became subject to the global 10% Section 122 tariff surcharge.
